findings.description: The retention / purge section states the run reaches a terminal state of 'COMPLETED, FAILED, or CANCELLED'. The canonical run_status_enum has no COMPLETED value; the correct terminal success state for workflow_runs is FINALIZED.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'COMPLETED' with 'FINALIZED' in the terminal-state list so it reads 'FINALIZED, FAILED, or CANCELLED'.

findings.description: The purge-after-run-completion section states the run transitions to a terminal state of 'COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED'. The canonical run_status_enum has no COMPLETED value; the correct terminal success state for workflow_runs is FINALIZED.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'COMPLETED' with 'FINALIZED' in the terminal-state list so it reads 'FINALIZED, FAILED, CANCELLED'.

findings.description: All 8 gate function names in the IN_MONTHLY phase table use the deprecated 3-part gate.in.<descriptor> pattern (e.g. gate.in.ingestion_complete). The decisions_log 2026-05-15 amendment mandates engine.gate_<phase_descriptor> (2-part, engine namespace) as the canonical form; gate_function_library_schema.md was updated but this file was not.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename all gate references to use engine.gate_<descriptor> (e.g. gate.in.ingestion_complete → engine.gate_ingestion_complete) throughout the file.

findings.description: Gate references gate.out.matching_complete (line 11) and gate.out.manual_upload_hold_clear (lines 97, 112) use the deprecated 3-part pattern. Canonical form per decisions_log is engine.gate_matching_complete and engine.gate_manual_upload_hold_clear.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace gate.out.matching_complete with engine.gate_matching_complete and gate.out.manual_upload_hold_clear with engine.gate_manual_upload_hold_clear throughout the file.

findings.description: Multiple references to gate.finalization.preconditions_satisfied, gate.finalization.approval_recorded, and related gate.finalization.* names use the deprecated 3-part pattern. Canonical form is engine.gate_finalization_preconditions_satisfied, engine.gate_approval_recorded, etc.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace all gate.finalization.<descriptor> references with engine.gate_<descriptor> throughout the file.

findings.description: All 8 finalization gate definitions and the composite gate use the deprecated gate.finalization.<descriptor> 3-part naming (e.g. gate.finalization.no_open_blocking_issues, gate.finalization.preconditions_satisfied). Canonical form is engine.gate_<descriptor>.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename all gate.finalization.<descriptor> references to engine.gate_<descriptor> (e.g. gate.finalization.no_open_blocking_issues → engine.gate_no_open_blocking_issues) throughout the file.

findings.description: The composite gate gate.finalization.adjustment_preconditions_satisfied and its 4 sub-gates (gate.finalization.adjustment_parent_finalized etc.) use the deprecated 3-part pattern.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename all gate.finalization.adjustment_* references to engine.gate_adjustment_* throughout the file.

findings.description: All gate function names in the OUT_MONTHLY phase table use the deprecated gate.out.<descriptor> pattern (e.g. gate.out.ingestion_complete, gate.out.matching_complete). Canonical form is engine.gate_<descriptor>.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename all gate.out.<descriptor> references to engine.gate_<descriptor> throughout the file.

findings.description: All gate function names in the IN_MONTHLY phase table use the deprecated gate.in.<descriptor> pattern (e.g. gate.in.ingestion_complete, gate.in.income_matching_complete). Canonical form is engine.gate_<descriptor>.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename all gate.in.<descriptor> references to engine.gate_<descriptor> throughout the file.

findings.description: The registration example in the Cross-block contract section uses gate_name: 'gate.out.matching_complete', which is the deprecated 3-part pattern. The canonical registration form is 'engine.gate_matching_complete'.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the gate_name in the registration example to 'engine.gate_matching_complete' to reflect the current canonical naming.

findings.description: The gate is identified as gate.finalization.audit_log_quiescent throughout the file, using the deprecated 3-part pattern. Canonical form is engine.gate_audit_log_quiescent.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace gate.finalization.audit_log_quiescent with engine.gate_audit_log_quiescent throughout the file.

findings.description: Reference to gate.finalization.preconditions_satisfied uses the deprecated 3-part pattern. Canonical form is engine.gate_finalization_preconditions_satisfied.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace gate.finalization.preconditions_satisfied with engine.gate_finalization_preconditions_satisfied.

findings.description: Reference to gate.out.matching_complete uses the deprecated 3-part pattern. Canonical form is engine.gate_matching_complete.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace gate.out.matching_complete with engine.gate_matching_complete.

findings.description: Cross-reference to gate.in.income_matching_complete uses the deprecated 3-part pattern. Canonical form is engine.gate_income_matching_complete.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace gate.in.income_matching_complete with engine.gate_income_matching_complete.

findings.description: Reference to gate.finalization.preconditions uses the deprecated 3-part pattern. Canonical form is engine.gate_finalization_preconditions (or engine.gate_finalization_preconditions_satisfied).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace gate.finalization.preconditions with engine.gate_finalization_preconditions_satisfied.

findings.description: The event INTEGRATION_REPLAY_DRIFT_DETECTED is referenced as the test failure signal at line 74, but this event name is not present in audit_event_taxonomy.md. The taxonomy contains LIVE_TEST_DRIFT_DETECTED (listed at line 129 of the same file) for the same semantic.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace INTEGRATION_REPLAY_DRIFT_DETECTED with the canonical LIVE_TEST_DRIFT_DETECTED on line 74.

findings.description: The event EVENT_AUDIT_RECOVERED is referenced as the generic recovery event for non-finalization audit-append failures, but this event name is not present in audit_event_taxonomy.md. Only FINALIZATION_LOCK_AUDIT_RECOVERED is catalogued.
findings.suggested_fix: Either add EVENT_AUDIT_RECOVERED to audit_event_taxonomy.md under the WORKFLOW domain, or clarify that the generic recovery path reuses FINALIZATION_LOCK_AUDIT_RECOVERED with a domain override.

findings.description: CREATE TYPE credit_note_status_enum is defined here as ('ISSUED', 'VOIDED') but the canonical definition in credit_note_schema.md is ('DRAFT', 'ISSUED', 'VOIDED'). The DRAFT value is missing in the cumulative_cap schema, creating an inconsistent enum that would fail at migration time.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the duplicate CREATE TYPE credit_note_status_enum from credit_note_cumulative_cap_schema.md and add a cross-reference to credit_note_schema.md for the authoritative enum definition.

findings.description: CREATE TABLE credit_notes is partially redefined here with a different column set (e.g. amount_eur_cents as bigint vs amount_eur as numeric(15,2) in credit_note_schema.md; missing voided_at / voided_by_user_id; missing status transitions). This creates a contradictory DDL specification for the same table.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the CREATE TABLE credit_notes block from credit_note_cumulative_cap_schema.md; replace with a cross-reference to credit_note_schema.md and document only the cumulative-cap constraint as an additional CHECK or trigger.

findings.description: CREATE TYPE dedup_status_enum is defined here as ('ACCEPTED', 'HARD_DUPLICATE', 'SOFT_DUPLICATE'), which is the conceptual 3-value form. The canonical operational definition in transactions_schema.md uses ('NEW', 'DUPLICATE_EXACT', 'DUPLICATE_POSSIBLE', 'NEEDS_REVIEW'). Two CREATE TYPE statements with the same name and different values will fail at migration time.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove or rename the dedup_status_enum definition in deduplication_fingerprint_schema.md; use a clearly named conceptual alias (e.g. dedup_policy_outcome) or provide only narrative documentation of the mapping.

findings.description: CREATE TYPE run_status_enum is defined here with the same 10 values as in workflow_run_schema.md. The reference doc is the canonical descriptive home for the enum but should not contain a redundant CREATE TYPE DDL that duplicates the schema file.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the CREATE TYPE DDL block from workflow_state_enum.md and keep only the narrative enum table; add a note that the authoritative DDL lives in workflow_run_schema.md.

findings.description: Cross-reference labels match_records_schema as a 'Layer 2 forward reference', but the file match_record_schema.md now exists in Docs/sub/schemas/.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the '(Block 04 Phase 03 — Layer 2 forward reference)' qualifier and update the reference to point to the existing match_record_schema.md.

findings.description: Cross-reference labels adjustment_record_schema as a '(forward reference)', but the file adjustment_record_schema.md now exists in Docs/sub/schemas/.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the '(forward reference)' qualifier from the adjustment_record_schema cross-reference.

findings.description: Cross-reference labels adjustment_record_schema as a '(forward reference)', but the file adjustment_record_schema.md now exists in Docs/sub/schemas/.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the '(forward reference)' qualifier from the adjustment_record_schema cross-reference.

findings.description: States that generated export files are retained for 30 days in the Processing zone. The canonical data_retention_policy mandates Export temp zone (bucket: export-temp) with a 24-hour TTL enforced by Supabase Storage lifecycle rules — not 30 days in the Processing zone.
findings.suggested_fix: Change line 60 to state that export files are staged in the export-temp bucket with a 24-hour TTL per data_retention_policy; remove the reference to Processing zone and 30-day retention for export files.
